text.title: LLM Instrumentation Rules, Alerts, and Cost Tracking
text.summary: Instrumentation rules, production alert thresholds, cost tracking patterns, agent tracing gotchas, and antipatterns.

rule.statement: Attach user_id, session_id, and feature_flag to every LLM span at call time — not post-hoc.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc enrichment requires a join on a secondary store. Inline attribution enables per-user cost breakdown, session replay, and A/B comparison directly in the tracing UI.

rule.statement: Log both input and output token counts per call; never derive cost from character count alone.
rule.rationale: Character count diverges from token count for non-ASCII content, code, and structured JSON. Token counts are available directly from provider responses and are the only reliable cost signal.

list.item: Wrap every external LLM call in a span; include model name, provider, temperature, max_tokens
For streaming responses, record TTFT (time to first token) as a separate metric
Record cache hit/miss on every call to track prompt caching effectiveness
For agents: create parent span per agent run, child spans per tool call; include iteration count

rule.statement: Alert on these baselines: error rate >5%, P99 latency >10s, TTFT P95 >2s, hallucination rate >10%, daily spend >80% of budget.
rule.rationale: These thresholds represent practical production baselines. Lower thresholds create alert fatigue; higher thresholds miss meaningful degradation. Tune to your SLA after establishing a 2-week baseline.

list.item: Cost spike: hourly spend >150% of 7-day rolling average → warning
Quality drop: average quality score <3.5/5 sustained for 15 minutes → warning
Cache hit rate <20% for 30 minutes → info (investigate prompt variation)
All critical alerts → PagerDuty; warnings → Slack #llm-alerts channel
Use pricing.yaml to centralize model costs — never hardcode rates in application code
Track cost by team, environment, and feature via span attributes; bill back to owners
Budget allocation: production 70%, staging 20%, development 10% of monthly budget
Self-hosted break-even: ~30,000 minutes/month (500 GPU-hours); below that use APIs

rule.statement: Create a parent trace per agent invocation and nest all tool calls as child spans — never emit flat independent spans.
rule.rationale: Flat spans make it impossible to reconstruct reasoning chains or correlate tool results with the LLM decision that triggered them. Hierarchical spans enable full session replay.

list.item: Record tool_name, input_args, output_size, and success/error for every tool call span
Log intermediate reasoning steps (thought content) as span events, not separate spans
Detect loops: flag when the same tool is called with identical args >2 times in one session
Store full conversation history in trace metadata for debugging hallucination root causes

antipattern.description: Sampling all traces at <100% in development.
antipattern.reason: Development traffic is low enough to sample at 100%. Sampling misses the exact anomalous call you need to debug. Reserve sampling strategies (e.g., 10%) for high-volume production only.

antipattern.description: Using Prometheus counters for LLM cost without per-model labels.
antipattern.reason: A single llm_cost_usd counter masks model mix. If GPT-4o spikes while GPT-4o-mini stays flat, an unlabeled counter hides the root cause. Always include model and provider labels.

antipattern.description: Only alerting on error rate, ignoring quality score drift.
antipattern.reason: Quality regressions (relevance drop, hallucination increase) do not surface as HTTP errors. They show up as user churn. Quality alerts require LLM-as-judge sampling running continuously in production.

text.reference: Prometheus alert rules implementing all thresholds above
Model pricing config for cost calculation
